channel.title: Haera Wiki
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Humanoid
item.description:
Humanoids are a creature type, ranging from small sizes (2-4 feet tall) to medium (4-8 feet tall). Here are some humanoids in the land of Haera:
Aasimar
Dragonborn
Dwarf
Elf
Gnome
Goliath
Halfling
Human
Orc
Tiefling

item.title: The School of Ego
item.description:
The School of Ego is an established college primarily for wizards and sorcerers, where they work together to formulate magical apparatus, spells and techniques. They are based in the city of Ego in Northern Haera.Notable individuals:
Professor Forbur Wilkinberry
Professor Seradine Blackwood Establishments found in:
Altrae
Ego
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Reykja
Thawnia

item.title: Government of Faeria
item.description:
Faeria is run by the Government of Faeria. It was first introduced after The Warring Times where the acting monarch, Queen Farlyn Mysth descended the throne to aid war efforts.
In The Modern Era, the government manages establishments across the city, as well as funding The People's Shield and the Adventurer's Guild.Establishments found in:
Faeria

item.title: Fleetfoot Post
item.description:
Fleetfoot Post is a postage and delivery service primarily based in Ego. In collaboration with The School of Ego, Fleetfoot has developed magic-assisted postage systems that can produce temporary portals for pigeon carriers using stored magic (not to be confused with the theory of Stored Magic) acquired from low-energy magic crystals.
Fleetfoot's magical postage system has garnered great attention, and is seen as the best postage service in Haera. Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell and more...

item.title: Reykja
item.description: Reykja is a city in Northern Haera. It is located to the northeast of Soarre and west of Orhum.Establishments based in Reykja:

item.title: The People's Shield
item.description:
The People's Shield is a guard force deployed across all of Haera, but based in Faeria. It aims to protect towns and cities from monster attacks, and regularly patrols across the continent to answer nearby emergencies if required.
The general public appreciate the Shield's protection, and share positive views for the guard force. The Shield is largely funded by the Government of Faeria, but also earns money via dungeon deployments or cross-continent protection work. Establishment found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell and more...

item.title: Seaside Trading Guild
item.description:
"What's yours can be mine, to exact your price divined." Motto of the Seaside Trading Guild The Seaside Trading Guild is a trading and merchant's guild based in Altrae of Eastern Haera. Though Altrae is on the eastern coast of Haera, the guild is very active and prominent in Western Haera, especially the coastal cities Faeria and XX. The Seaside Trading Guild is known to operate generously with merchants and traders in cities across Haera, and often rents guildhall spaces to other local guilds, such as local mason, leatherworker, farmer and fishermen guilds. Notable individuals:
Elaine Ridgewater
Gor Schmidt
Hamish Rippletook Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dragontail
Ego
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Reykja
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell and more...

item.title: Adventurer's Guild
item.description:
The Adventurer's Guild is a far-reaching establishment that supports adventurers across Haera, primarily based in Faeria. It is responsible for contracting adventurers for jobs and quests, and often provides supplies and/or resources for sale to aid them in their adventures.
Hero's Hilt is also an important contributor for the Guild, and is well-endowed with standard adventurer supplies. Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Orhum
Reykja
The Kingdom of Marcell more...

item.title: Scholars of Thought
item.description:
The Scholars of Thought are a collection of philosophers, historians and theorists well known throughout Haera. The Scholars have extensive reach across the continent, with primary bases in Ego, Faeria and Altrae. Notable individuals:
Queen Masja Alonzo Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Ego
Faeria
Reykja
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: Kinsguard
item.description:
The Kinsguard (not Kingsguard) is a guard force primarily trained and based in The Kingdom of Marcell. It was first introduced by Queen Amelia Lukeheart during The Classical Era.
Though not strictly bound to The Kingdom of Marcell, the Kinsguard usually exclusively operates to protect the citizens of Marcell from monstrous threats. Seldomly, the Kinsguard may be deployed to protect important carriages as they journey across Haera. Establishments found in:
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: House of Alonzo
item.description:
The House of Alonzo is a prevalent House in Abstract, but despite its ties to the Abstract monarchy, is a well-established and respected organization that researches and develops mechanical devices, with members from the Scholars of Thought, and primarily Dwarf, Halfling, Human and Aasimar members.
Before the passing of Juan Alonzo, Queen Masja Alonzo was a prominent and valued council member of the House.Establishments found in:
Abstract

item.title: Abstract
item.description:
Abstract is a kingdom consisting of smaller towns and cities in Eastern Haera that is located near Renavoir. It is home to many humans, halflings, and other humanoids, and one of the last regions on Haera that is still run by a monarch.
The current monarch is Queen Masja Alonzo, who has reigned for the past three years since the death of her brother Juan Alonzo. Monarchs:
Queen Masja Alonzo (current, The Modern Era)
Juan Alonzo (The Modern Era) Establishments based in Abstract:
House of Alonzo

item.title: Orhum
item.description: Orhum is a city in Northern Haera. It is located to the north of Soarre and northwest of Reykja.Establishments based in Orhum:

item.title: Northern Cities
item.description:
The Northern Cities consist of various settlements in Northern Haera, including:
Ego
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia

item.title: Dragontail
item.description: Dragontail is a city in Central Haera to the south of Landspine. Establishments based in Dragontail:

item.title: Central Haera
item.description:
Central Haera is a region of lush, green forests accompanied by large hills, mountain ranges, and a marsh, located in the center of Haera.It consists of:
Anecdote
Canvas
Dragontail
Everbrook
Landspine
Split
Stockold

item.title: Faeria
item.description:
Faeria is a coastal city in Western Haera to the north of Lune and west of Raea. It is neighbored by a small forest, and is the largest city in Haera. It is home to humans, halflings, elves and other humanoids. Establishments based in Faeria:
Adventurer's Guild
Government of Faeria
Scholars of Thought
The People's Shield

item.title: Western Haera
item.description:
Western Haera is a region of vast plains, small forests with a small selection of mountain ranges, located in the west of Haera. It consists of:
Drakehidt
Faeria
Landspine
Lune
Raea
Windswept Plains

item.title: Ego
item.description:
Ego is a city in Northern Haera to the south of Ergo. It is home to a wide range of humanoids, primarily humans, gnomes, and some dragonborns. Establishments based in Ego:
Fleetfoot Post
Scholars of Thought
The School of Ego

item.title: King Arthur Lukeheart II
item.description:
"Soon, a great peril will fall upon the Kingdom. Evil incarnate, in the form of a devastating dragon will strike Marcell. It is by defeating this malignant creature that my son will be crowned King." Prophecy by Arthur Lukeheart three years before the incident.
King Arthur Lukeheart II is the current Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell. He is a valiant warrior in battle, having conquered many foes in regional battles mostly around Upper Central Haera. His late father, Arthur Lukeheart deemed his child will be destined for kingship after slaying a mighty dragon, which ultimately occurred at the cost of several lives, including that of his father.
item.guid: Characters/Non Playable Characters/King Arthur Lukeheart II.md

item.title: Juan Alonzo
item.description:
"He radiated celestial light to pave the path for others throughout his orbit. May he now rest in peace." Queen Masja Alonzo in the public funeral of Juan Alonzo.
Juan Alonzo was the Aasimar king of Abstract and died at age 54 during a battle near Landspine. In life, Alonzo was a strong ruler who was fair and just. He was a powerful Paladin, but lacked inventive creativity, hence did not participate in the proceedings of the House of Alonzo.

item.title: Everbrook
item.description: Everbrook is a group of small towns in Central Haera, bordering Anecdote on its east. Establishments based in Everbrook:

item.title: Anecdote
item.description: Anecdote is a city in Central Haera, bordering Everbrook to its west. Establishments based in Anecdote:

item.title: Intrigue
item.description: Intrigue is a mysterious island in Southern Haera. It boasts an odd geological appearance, with towering mountains and peaks surrounded by quaint palm trees. Generations of monks and philosophers have travelled to Intrigue to seek inner peace or training, similar to the Forsaken Perch.Its exotic palm trees produce various species of interesting fruit, often sold for very high prices due to its remoteness and exquisite taste.

item.title: Renavoir
item.description: Renavoir is a rainforest situated in the south of Eastern Haera. It is home to many exotic animals, species and fruit. Its density and high humidity has made it difficult for cartographers to map out the region properly over the years, and many parts of it remain unexplored.Many adventurers have attempted to find hidden treasure in Renavoir, though thus far none have returned successful, or returned at all.

item.title: Hero's Feast
item.description:
Hero's Feast is a Halfling tavern chain primarily based in Hero's Hilt, founded during The Classical Era by Chardine Suave. Signature dishes: Grilled giant chicken's leg and titan potato soup Deluxe steak and titan potato fries Cheesy lobster and prawn (coastal establishments only) Hero's Feast (Hero's Hilt only) salads procured from regional vegetables titan potato soup multiple species of bird and land animals served in various techniques: grilled, fried, baked with titan potato fries
exotic fruits from Renavoir and Intrigue. Hero's Feast locations:
Hero's Hilt
Abstract
Altrae
Faeria
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: Chardine Suave
item.description: Chardine Suave is a Halfling who founded Hero's Feast in Hero's Hilt. He was a wonderful chef whose spectacular culinary expertise was praised for many years, even after his passing.

item.title: Hero's Hilt
item.description:
Hero's Hilt is a city located in the south-east end of Split. Its name originates from the belief that a mighty hero's blade created the canyon between the mountains of Split. It primarily consists of halflings and is a major hub for the Adventurer's Guild. Establishments based in Hero's Hilt:
Adventurer's Guild
Hero's Feast

item.title: Canvas
item.description:
"Home to plenty of magical kin, magical forests and a magical lake. Truly a remarkable geographical sight." Prof. Wilkinberry, The School of Ego
Canvas is a very large forest in Central Haera, bordering Stockold, Sandspine and Split. It is home to many elves, druids and...Canvas revolves around an enormous lake which is said to provide healing properties if processed correctly with the correct materials.

item.title: Stored Magic
item.description:
"A breakthrough in Stored Magic could vastly improve strength and power of guards and armies, greatly heightening their performance against monstrous threats." Professor Forbur Wilkinberry.
Stored magic is a magical theory by Forbur Wilkinberry and Seradine Blackwood that attunement to magic items requires a "magically-tangible" manifestation of the user's soul, thus aligning one's soul to the corresponding item.
Wilkinberry and Blackwood theorised that through capturing and replicating behaviors of a user's soul, it may be possible to duplicate the effect of attunement with magical apparatus, potentially allowing for rapid attunement switching and magic item use in combat scenarios. However, the theory is currently facing opposition in The School of Ego as success in this theory could become very dangerous in the wrong hands.

item.title: Southern Haera
item.description:
Southern Haera is a region largely consisting of deserts, mountains and life-less lands, in the south of Haera.It consists of:
Blighted Lands
Egia Desert
Eror
Forsaken Perch
Greyhorde

item.title: Queen Masja Alonzo
item.description:
Queen Masja Alonzo is the current Aasimar monarch of Abstract. Before she became Queen, she was a notable inventor in the House of Alonzo, developing many trinkets and devices helpful to the general public.
She was a prevalent council member of the Scholars of Thought, and had to step down after the passing of her brother Juan Alonzo three years ago. She is an amiable leader and members of Abstract are content with her ruling.

item.title: Eastern Haera
item.description:
Eastern Haera is a region of temperate forests, plains, and a rainforest in the east of Haera. It consists of:
Abstract
Altrae
Merphos
Renavoir

item.title: Northwind
item.description: Northwind is a region within Upper Central Haera, covering a significant portion of the area's cold forests. It contains numerous small settlements, towns and minor cities.

item.title: Upper Central Haera
item.description:
Upper Central Haera is a region of cold plains and forests in the northern center of Haera, consisting of:
Conduit
Northwind
Sandspine
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: History of Haera
item.description:
The history of Haera is largely defined by four eras, in chronological order: The Forgotten Times
The Classical Era
The Warring Times
The Modern Era

item.title: The Forgotten Times
item.description:
The Forgotten Times is the earliest era in the History of Haera, and only fragmented knowledge of this era is known, due to extensive monster activity in many regions, making it difficult for regions of Haera to communicate with each other. It is thought that only high-ranking families and clans have access to ancestral records referencing potential events and recollections of this era.
Though the Scholars of Thought have repeatedly attempted to acquire information about The Forgotten Times, every known family or clan have turned them down, potentially due to some of the contents presented in such records.
The Classical Era succeeds this era.

item.title: Haera
item.description:
Haera is a continent in the Material Plane, home to a diverse range of humanoids, such as humans or elves. Regions of Haera:
Central Haera
Eastern Haera
Northern Haera
Outer Haera
Southern Haera
Upper Central Haera
Western Haera

item.title: Altrae
item.description:
Altrae is a city in Eastern Haera that is located near Merphos. It is a coastal city, home to many humans, halflings, and other humanoids. Establishments based in Altrae:
Seaside Trading Guild
Scholars of Thought

item.title: Outer Haera
item.description:
Outer Haera consists of locations and regions disconnected from the main continent of Haera.It consists of:
Aethar
Blood Frosts
Chromatia
Intrigue
Obsidian Peaks

item.title: Split
item.description:
Split is a small mountain range in Central Haera with a river that flows directly through the mountains.
It is rumored that in The Forgotten Times, a powerful demi-god or hero slashed through the mountain range with a powerful greatsword during a conflict.

item.title: Landspine
item.description: Landspine is a vast multi-directional mountain range that borders Western Haera, Central Haera and the mountains of Drakehidt.

item.title: The Kingdom of Marcell
item.description:
The Kingdom of Marcell consists of numerous cities in Upper Central Haera to the west of Sandspine. It is one of the last regions on Haera that is still run by a monarch.
The current monarch is King Arthur Lukeheart II, who has participated in numerous regional battles in the past. Monarchs:
King Arthur Lukeheart II (current, The Modern Era)
Arthur Lukeheart (The Modern Era)
Amelia Lukeheart (The Classical Era)
Vasir Lukeheart (The Classical Era) Establishments based in The Kingdom of Marcell:
Kinsguard

item.title: Vasir Lukeheart
item.description: Vasir Lukeheart was a Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell during The Classical Era. He was assassinated at age 47 during his reign after he caused numerous incidents that outraged his people and plummeted the reputation of the Kinsguard, who he bribed and used to selectively protect particular cities within Marcell, leaving the rest to fend for themselves against devastating attacks and raids.

item.title: Amelia Lukeheart
item.description:
Amelia Lukeheart was a prominent and famed Human Queen of The Kingdom of Marcell during The Classical Era. Amelia passed peacefully at the age of 66, handing the crown to her son Vasir.
She introduced the Kinsguard to Marcell, and was the first Queen of Marcell since The Forgotten Times.

item.title: Conduit Travel Theory
item.description:
Conduit Travel Theory (CTT) is a theory based on the magical energy lingering around the mountains of Conduit. Through years of study and research, schools and magic-related groups have arrived to this theory.CTT suggests that the lingering magical energy can be manipulated and 'bound' to certain planes, and this bond can be enhanced with the magical energy of a user. Warlocks have been extensively invited to Conduit for the testing of this theory, due to their inter-planar connections with their patrons, but results are neither confirming nor denying the theory, as there is currently no method to determine whether the bond has been formed.The primary contributors to CTT are: warlock
The School of Ego
The Circle of Ley Lines

item.title: Conduit
item.description:
Conduit is a group of mountains that emerge from a vast lake towards the west of Upper Central Haera.
Schools of wizardry, sorcery, churches and druids have studied Conduit for many years, and it is widely theorised that the mountains are host to very high magical energy, and if correct calibration and an inter-planar connection is made, it may be possible to harness the energy of Conduit to travel to other planes. See more in Conduit Travel Theory.

item.title: Arthur Lukeheart
item.description: Arthur Lukeheart (not to be confused with King Arthur Lukeheart II) was the Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell and died at age 72, being defeated by an evil dragon later slain by his son, King Arthur Lukeheart II. In life, Lukeheart occasionally had bursts of prophetic vision, which has influenced his ruling over the course of his kingship. Citizens were generally amiable towards his use of his strange power, and did not attempt to thwart his reign as he was a wise king whose tactical and strategic knowledge was greatly valued in battle, and was considerably generous to the common people.

item.title: Hamish Rippletook
item.description:
Hamish Rippletook is the acting director of the Seaside Trading Guild Guildhall based in Faeria. He is a Gnome. Though Faeria is on the opposite side of Haera to Altrae, Rippletook is a reliable ally of Elaine Ridgewater since before she became the leader of the guild.
Rippletook is often seen performing trade in the public market, protected by bodyguards of The People's Shield.

item.title: Gor Schmidt
item.description: Gor Schmidt is the second-in-command of the Seaside Trading Guild. He is a Human based in Altrae. Though Schmidt works under Elaine Ridgewater, he is seen more often in public areas than her, taking over minor dealings in her place or investigating and recruiting incoming merchants.Schmidt is often seen as a bodyguard for Ridgewater in important transactions, and it is well-known that he was previously an adventurer as a Rogue.

item.title: Elaine Ridgewater
item.description:
Elaine Ridgewater is the current owner and leader of the Seaside Trading Guild. She is a Halfling based in Altrae and is rarely seen outside of the Seaside Trading Guildhall. It is said that she orchestrates some of the most influential and important trades in the city of Altrae, and may be involved in numerous dealings in nearby regions.
Her second-in-command is Gor Schmidt, who can often be seen alongside her during big trade discussions and transactions.

item.title: Thawnia
item.description: Thawnia is a city in Northern Haera. It is home to Dragonborns, ...Establishments based in Thawnia:

item.title: Stockold
item.description: Stockold is a mountain range in Central Haera that is located near Canvas.

item.title: Seradine Blackwood
item.description: Seradine Blackwood is the wife of Forbur Wilkinberry, and a highly renowned Human Sorcerer in the The School of Ego. She pioneered the development and theory of Stored Magic with her husband, paving the path forwards to attunement-less magic item use.

item.title: Forbur Wilkinberry
item.description: Forbur Wilkinberry is a Gnome professor in The School of Ego who is regarded as one of the greatest wizard academics of The Modern Era, having invented numerous items that store and contain magic, and pioneered the development and theory of Stored Magic along with his wife Seradine Blackwood.

item.title: The Modern Era
item.description: The Modern Era is the current era in which Haera lies. It succeeds The Warring Times in the History of Haera.

item.title: Ergo
item.description:
Ergo is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It lies to the east of Soarre and to the north of Snowspine.
It is speculated by the The School of Ego that the highest point in Ergo leads to the Upper Planes, or contains great compatibility for inter-planar travel.

item.title: Merphos
item.description: Merphos is a vast forest in Eastern Haera home to many elves, druids, ....

item.title: Sandspine
item.description: Sandspine is a vast mountain range that encompasses part of Churn and borders Merphos.

item.title: Churn
item.description: Churn is a desert region mostly surrounded by mountains in northeast Haera, consisting of...

item.title: Snowspine
item.description: Snowspine is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It borders Ergo to the north and Schmine to the southwest.

item.title: Soarre
item.description: Soarre is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It lies to the west of Ergo and to the north of Schmine.

item.title: Schmine
item.description: Schmine is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the central north of the continent. It lies on the border of Northern Haera and Upper Central Haera.

item.title: Northern Haera
item.description: Northern Haera is a region of snowy plains, forests and mountains in the north of Haera, consisting of the Northern Mountains and the Northern Cities.

item.title: Northern Mountains
item.description:
The Northern Mountains consist of various mountain ranges in the north of Haera, including:
Ergo
Schmine
Snowspine
Soarre

item.title: Inner Planes
item.description:
The Inner Planes are Planes consisting of:
Elemental Plane of Air
Elemental Plane of Earth
Elemental Plane of Fire
Elemental Plane of Water
Elemental Chaos
Para-Elemental Planes

item.title: Ethereal Plane
item.description: The Ethereal Plane is a Plane in the Transitive Planes, ...

item.title: Astral Plane
item.description: The Astral Plane is a Plane in the Transitive Planes, ...

item.title: Aasimar
item.description: Aasimar are humanoids who carry a spark of the Upper Planes within their souls. They can be descended from angelic beings or infused with Celestial power, fanning that spark to bring light, healing and heavenly fury.They can be of medium or small size, with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Transitive Planes
item.description:
Transitive planes serve as paths between planes, including: Astral Plane
Ethereal Plane

item.title: Shadowfell
item.description: The Shadowfell is a Plane in The Material Realms, home to grim wastelands, haunted ruins and the Domains of Dread.

item.title: Material Plane
item.description:
The material plane is a Plane in the The Material Realms, home to many humanoids, such as humans and elves.
One notable continent in the material plane is Haera.

item.title: Feywild
item.description: The Feywild is a Plane in The Material Realms, home to...

item.title: The Material Realms
item.description:
The Material Realms consist of Planes including:
Material Plane
Feywild
Shadowfell

item.title: Plane
item.description:
A plane of existence. Planes include: The Material Realms
Transitive Planes
Inner Planes
Outer Planes

item.title: Lower Planes
item.description:
The lower planes are a subset of the Outer Planes consisting of:
Abyss
Acheron
Carceri
Gehenna
Hades
Nine Hells
Pandemonium

item.title: Outer Planes
item.description:
The Outer Planes are Planes consisting of:
Abyss
Acheron
Arborea
Arcadia
Beastlands
Bytopia
Carceri
Elysium
Gehenna
Hades
Limbo
Mechanus
Mount Celestia
Nine Hells
Outlands
Pandemonium
Ysgard Two subsets of the Outer Planes include:
Lower Planes
Upper Planes

item.title: Upper Planes
item.description:
The Upper Planes are a subset of the Outer Planes consisting of:
Arborea
Arcadia
Beastlands
Bytopia
Elysium
Mount Celestia
Ysgard

item.title: Goliath
item.description: Goliaths are...

item.title: Tiefling
item.description: Tieflings are...

item.title: Orc
item.description: Orcs are...

item.title: Halfling
item.description: Halflings are...

item.title: Gnome
item.description: Gnomes are...

item.title: Elf
item.description: Elves are...

item.title: Dwarf
item.description: Dwarves are...

item.title: Dragonborn
item.description: Dragonborn are...

item.title: Human
item.description: Humans are...

item.title: Dragon
item.description: Dragons are...
